---
title: "About"
page-layout: article
---

## Hi, I'm Anirudh Jain

I'm a PhD student at **Aalto University** (Helsinki), supervised by [Dr. Samuel Kaski](https://people.aalto.fi/samuel.kaski), and an **ML/AI Scientist at Orion Pharma**, where I work on applying machine learning to drug discovery.

My research sits at the intersection of probabilistic machine learning, molecular design, and Bayesian deep learning. Over the course of my PhD I have worked on:

- **Graph generative models** for de novo molecular design, with a focus on conditional generation given user-specified properties (e.g. activity, selectivity, synthetic accessibility).
- **Multi-target predictive models** for molecular property prediction across protein targets.
- **Latent space exploration** — developing more efficient strategies for navigating the latent spaces of graph-based generative models.
- **Scalable approximate Bayesian methods** for neural networks on large datasets (the subject of my Master's thesis).

More recently, my interest has shifted toward **uncertainty quantification** in the context of active learning for molecular property prediction — which is what this blog is about.

## This Blog

I write about probabilistic machine learning methods applied to molecular property prediction and drug discovery. Posts tend to be practical and benchmark-driven: I implement something, run it on real data, and report what actually happens.

Topics include uncertainty quantification, active learning, Gaussian processes, deep kernel learning, and graph neural networks for chemistry.

## Contact

- 📧 [anirudh.jain@orionpharma.com](mailto:anirudh.jain@orionpharma.com)
- 🐙 [github.com/sponde25](https://github.com/sponde25)

---

*All views and opinions expressed on this blog are my own and do not represent the views of Orion Pharma or Aalto University.*
